slides.title: Describing Words
slides.content:
We use special words to describe animals and people:

🦁 fierce — scary and strong (lions are fierce)

😌 calm — quiet and not worried (the sea is calm today)

💪 strong — has a lot of power (elephants are strong)

🐰 gentle — soft and kind (rabbits are gentle)

🧠 smart — very clever (dolphins are smart)

slides.title: Adventure Vocabulary
slides.content:
Let's learn some words about adventures!

🗺️ adventure — an exciting trip or experience

🙈 hide — to go where nobody can see you

🦴 prehistoric — from a very, very long time ago (like dinosaurs!)

🔍 discover — to find something new
slides.tip: Prehistoric means 'before history was written down'. Dinosaurs are prehistoric animals!

slides.title: Adverbs of Frequency
slides.content:
Adverbs of frequency tell us HOW OFTEN something happens:

✅✅✅ always — 100% of the time
✅✅ usually — most of the time
✅ often — many times
⚡ sometimes — a few times
❌ never — 0% of the time

They go before the main verb: I always eat breakfast.
slides.tip: Think of a scale from 0% to 100%: never → sometimes → often → usually → always

slides.title: Where Does the Adverb Go?
slides.problem:
Put the adverb in the correct place:

1. I ___ brush my teeth. (always)
2. She ___ eats fish. (never)
3. We ___ play in the park. (sometimes)
steps: 1️⃣ The adverb goes BEFORE the verb → I always brush my teeth.
2️⃣ Before the verb → She never eats fish.
3️⃣ Before the verb → We sometimes play in the park.
slides.answer: 1. I always brush my teeth. 2. She never eats fish. 3. We sometimes play in the park.

slides.title: Leo and Lily's Adventure
slides.html:
Leo and Lily were two little mice who lived in a big forest. One day, they decided to go on an adventure.

They walked and walked until they saw a river. A friendly frog said: 'I always swim across!' The frog gave them a big leaf to use as a boat.

On the other side, they met a fierce cat! Lily was calm. 'Let's hide!' she whispered. They hid behind a prehistoric rock.

The cat walked away. Leo said: 'I will never forget this adventure!'

slides.title: Describing Animals
slides.content:
We can describe animals using adjectives:

🐘 Elephants are strong. They are very big.

🐰 Rabbits are gentle. They are soft too.

🦁 Lions are fierce. They sometimes fight to protect their babies.

🐬 Dolphins are smart. They can learn tricks.

🐊 Crocodiles are patient. They wait a long time for food.

slides.title: Choose the Right Adverb
slides.problem:
Complete with always, usually, sometimes or never:

1. Fish ___ live in water. (100%)
2. I ___ eat pizza for breakfast. (0%)
3. We ___ go to the park on Sundays. (most of the time)
slides.answer: 1. Fish always live in water. 2. I never eat pizza for breakfast. 3. We usually go to the park on Sundays.

slides.title: The Eagle and the Rabbit
slides.html:
High up in the mountains, there lived a fierce eagle. It always flew very fast with its strong wings.

Down in the valley, there lived a gentle rabbit. It usually stayed near its home.

One day, the rabbit was lost. The eagle saw it. But the eagle was not hungry. It sometimes helped other animals.

'Follow me!' said the eagle. It flew slowly so the rabbit could follow. The rabbit found its way home.

'Thank you!' said the rabbit. 'You are fierce but also very kind!'

slides.title: Adverbs with 'To Be'
slides.content:
With most verbs, the adverb goes before the verb:

I always eat breakfast. She never runs.

But with the verb to be (am, is, are), the adverb goes after:

I am always happy. ✅
She is usually calm. ✅
They are never late. ✅
slides.tip: Remember: before normal verbs, but AFTER am/is/are!

slides.title: Before or After?
slides.problem:
Put the adverb in the right place:

1. He ___ is ___ happy. (always)
2. They ___ play ___ football. (often)
3. She ___ is ___ late. (never)
steps: 1️⃣ 'is' is the verb to be → adverb goes AFTER → He is always happy.
2️⃣ 'play' is a normal verb → adverb goes BEFORE → They often play football.
3️⃣ 'is' is the verb to be → adverb goes AFTER → She is never late.
slides.answer: 1. He is always happy. 2. They often play football. 3. She is never late.

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 🦁 Describing words: fierce (scary), calm (quiet), gentle (kind), smart (clever)
🗺️ Adventure words: adventure, hide, prehistoric, discover
🔄 Adverbs of frequency: always, usually, often, sometimes, never
📝 Adverbs go BEFORE normal verbs: I always eat breakfast
📝 Adverbs go AFTER 'to be': She is usually calm
⚡ Scale: never (0%) → sometimes → often → usually → always (100%)
